# improve-skill — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** improve-skill
**Generated by:** write-skill-eval
**Version:** 2.0

---

## Structural Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against the SKILL.md document itself. -->
<!-- These are evaluated by improve-skill WITHOUT running the skill. -->

- **EVAL-S01 — Baseline checklist:** From improve-skill's structural checklist (frontmatter third-person description with WHAT+WHEN, body < 500 lines, no time-sensitive content, consistent terminology, concrete examples, one-level file references, numbered workflows, POSIX paths, Progress Tracking section with TaskCreate examples, explicit `model:` on every Agent dispatch).

- **EVAL-S02 — Extended checklist:** No internal redundancy, DRY within body, good/bad contrast examples, prioritized input/decision points, filled-in format examples.

- **EVAL-S03 — Principles alignment:** Mental model present before mechanics, description is trigger-only, scope boundary stated with sibling skill named, instructions traceable to failure modes, policy/procedure balance in judgment-heavy steps, preconditions checked explicitly, token-budget boundary correct between always-on SKILL.md body and on-demand companion files.

- **EVAL-S04 — Flow-graph conformance:** MUST/MUST NOT/Entry/Flow sections present, node IDs explicit (`### [ID] Name`), per-node Don'ts co-located, exit conditions explicit with labeled edges, self-loops declared, [END] is a real node with Do/Don't, position tracking in MUST, per-node Load declarations at point of use.

- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** Wrong-tool redirect present
  - **Test:** SKILL.md contains a Wrong-Tool Detection section that names at least one case improve-skill does NOT handle and names the sibling skill (`/skill-creator` for new skills, `/write-skill-eval` for EVAL.md generation) as the correct alternative.
  - **Fail signal:** No explicit wrong-tool redirect exists anywhere in the body.

- [ ] **EVAL-S06:** EVAL.md auto-detection behavior is fully specified
  - **Test:** SKILL.md documents both branches of the EVAL.md check: (a) EVAL.md exists → use its criteria, (b) EVAL.md does not exist → offer to generate via `write-skill-eval` as an isolated subagent, with the reason for isolation stated (bias control).
  - **Fail signal:** Either branch is missing, or the isolation rationale is absent.

- [ ] **EVAL-S07:** Pass flags are documented with their effect
  - **Test:** `--structural-only` and `--behavioral-only` flags appear in the body with an explicit statement of which pass each skips via edge conditions in the flow graph.
  - **Fail signal:** Either flag is undocumented in the body, or the body documents the flags without showing their routing effect in Exit blocks.

- [ ] **EVAL-S08:** Execution-trace injection rule is unambiguous
  - **Test:** SKILL.md or its companion files state the exact condition under which the trace protocol is injected into subagent prompts: only when the target skill's EVAL.md contains an `## Execution Criteria` section. The negative case (no Execution Criteria → no injection) is also stated.
  - **Fail signal:** The condition for injection is absent, or only the positive case is stated with no explicit negative case.

- [ ] **EVAL-S09:** Structure-preserving edit rules are referenced, not inlined
  - **Test:** SKILL.md references `references/structure-preserving-edits.md` via a Load declaration at the fix node(s). The edit rules themselves are not duplicated inline in SKILL.md.
  - **Fail signal:** Structure-preserving edit rules are inlined in SKILL.md body, or no Load reference to the companion file exists.

- [ ] **EVAL-S10:** Precondition check verifies file references resolve
  - **Test:** The [P] node (or equivalent precondition step) includes a Do step that verifies all file references in the target skill directory resolve, with an explicit FAIL LOUDLY instruction for broken references.
  - **Fail signal:** No precondition step checks for broken symlinks or unresolvable Load targets, or the check exists without a loud failure instruction.

---

## Execution Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against HOW improve-skill behaves when run on a test prompt. -->
<!-- These are graded via execution traces returned by subagents. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-E01:** Progress tasks are created at the start before any pass executes
  - **Test:** The execution trace's `phases_executed` list begins with task creation. `TaskCreate` calls for Pass 1 and Pass 2 appear before any structural scoring or behavioral subagent dispatch.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows structural scoring or subagent dispatch before any TaskCreate calls, or TaskCreate calls are absent entirely.

- [ ] **EVAL-E02:** EVAL.md presence is checked before dispatching Pass 2 subagents
  - **Test:** The `precondition_checks` block in the trace includes an EVAL.md existence check with a pass/fail result. If the result is fail, the trace shows an offer to generate EVAL.md — not a silent skip or an error.
  - **Fail signal:** EVAL.md check is absent from the precondition block, or a missing EVAL.md causes Pass 2 to be silently skipped without an offer to generate.

- [ ] **EVAL-E03:** Pass 2 subagents are dispatched with an explicit model set
  - **Test:** Every `agents_dispatched` entry for behavioral test-prompt subagents in the trace has a non-empty `model` field. No dispatch uses a default or inherited model.
  - **Fail signal:** Any test-prompt subagent entry in the trace has a missing or empty `model` field.

- [ ] **EVAL-E04:** Trace injection occurs only when Execution Criteria exist in the target's EVAL.md
  - **Test:** The `workflow_decisions` block includes a decision for "trace injection" whose `chosen` value is "inject" if the target EVAL.md has an `## Execution Criteria` section, and "skip" if not.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace injection is applied unconditionally to all runs, or skipped unconditionally regardless of the target EVAL.md content.

- [ ] **EVAL-E05:** EVAL-O and EVAL-E grading tables are both produced when Execution Criteria exist
  - **Test:** When the target EVAL.md has an `## Execution Criteria` section, the trace's `phases_executed` list includes both an output-grading phase and an execution-grading phase with separate score card tables.
  - **Fail signal:** Only one grading table is produced when both EVAL-O and EVAL-E criteria exist; or the execution grading phase is absent from the trace.

- [ ] **EVAL-E06:** write-skill-eval is dispatched as an isolated subagent — not called in the parent context
  - **Test:** When EVAL.md is absent and the user accepts the offer to generate it, the `agents_dispatched` block shows a dispatch to `write-skill-eval` as a subagent with `context_isolation: true` (the parent's session context excluded from the subagent prompt).
  - **Fail signal:** `write-skill-eval` is invoked directly in the parent context (i.e., no Agent dispatch), or the dispatch includes session context from the parent's SKILL.md reading pass.

- [ ] **EVAL-E07:** Fix cycles halt and surface blockers when items cannot be resolved in two cycles
  - **Test:** The `workflow_decisions` block shows a max-cycle check after each pass. If two consecutive cycles fail to clear an item, the trace records a "surface blocker" decision rather than a third attempt.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows three or more fix cycles on the same failing item without a blocker surfaced to the user.

- [ ] **EVAL-E08:** skill-companion-file-writer dispatches carry an explicit model
  - **Test:** When the trace includes a `skill-companion-file-writer` entry in `agents_dispatched`, that entry has a non-empty `model` field. This applies to every companion file dispatch, not only Pass 2 dispatches.
  - **Fail signal:** Any `skill-companion-file-writer` entry in `agents_dispatched` has a missing or empty `model` field.

- [ ] **EVAL-E09:** skill-companion-file-writer subagents are dispatched in parallel when multiple files are needed
  - **Test:** When `agents_dispatched` contains two or more `skill-companion-file-writer` entries for the same fix cycle, the trace's `workflow_decisions` block records a "parallel dispatch" decision — not sequential dispatch.
  - **Fail signal:** Multiple companion file dispatches appear sequentially chained rather than parallel, or no dispatch decision is recorded when multiple companion files are required.

- [ ] **EVAL-E10:** --behavioral-only flag causes structural pass to be absent from phases_executed
  - **Test:** When invoked with `--behavioral-only`, the `phases_executed` list in the trace does not include the structural scoring phase or any structural fix cycle. The trace jumps from precondition check to behavioral pass.
  - **Fail signal:** The structural scoring phase or any structural edit appears in `phases_executed` when `--behavioral-only` is set.

- [ ] **EVAL-E11:** Prose structure detection is recorded as a precondition check
  - **Test:** The `precondition_checks` block in the trace includes a structure-detection entry (prose vs. flow-graph) with a pass/fail result. If prose is detected, the entry records that a structural finding was flagged and a redirect to `/skill-creator` was recommended.
  - **Fail signal:** `precondition_checks` has no structure-detection entry, or a prose-structured skill proceeds without any trace record of the detection and flag.

---

## Output Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against what improve-skill PRODUCES when run on a test prompt. -->
<!-- These are evaluated by improve-skill's behavioral pass — run skill, then grade output. -->

### Completeness

- [ ] **EVAL-O01:** Structural score card is present when Pass 1 runs
  - **Test:** Locate the output section covering structural results. Verify it contains: (a) a per-cycle tally with checklist domain labels (baseline / extended / EVAL-S / flow-graph), (b) a final pass rate after fixes, and (c) a named list of each failing item with a one-line reason.
  - **Fail signal:** No structural section present; or the section shows only a final score with no per-item failure list; or tallies are merged into a single undifferentiated number.

- [ ] **EVAL-O02:** Behavioral grading table is present when Pass 2 runs
  - **Test:** When the target skill has an EVAL.md with output criteria and test prompts, the output contains a per-prompt, per-criterion grading table (EVAL-Oxx columns, prompt rows, PASS/FAIL cells, cycle totals).
  - **Fail signal:** Behavioral pass ran but no grading table is produced, or the table lacks per-criterion columns.

- [ ] **EVAL-O03:** Execution grading table is present when EVAL-E criteria exist
  - **Test:** When the target EVAL.md contains an `## Execution Criteria` section, a separate execution grading table (EVAL-Exx columns) is included in the output alongside the EVAL-O table.
  - **Fail signal:** EVAL-E criteria exist but no execution grading table appears, or EVAL-E criteria are graded inside the EVAL-O table rather than separately.

- [ ] **EVAL-O04:** Every failing criterion has a traced root cause
  - **Test:** For every FAIL cell in any grading table, the output includes a trace entry showing: what failed, what was in the output, and which SKILL.md instruction caused the failure (or which instruction is absent).
  - **Fail signal:** Any FAIL appears in the grading table with no corresponding root-cause trace, or the trace names a symptom ("output is incomplete") without identifying the missing/defective instruction.

- [ ] **EVAL-O05:** Every fix is linked to a specific traced root cause
  - **Test:** For each edit made to SKILL.md during a fix cycle, the output names the EVAL-O or EVAL-E criterion that caused it and the root-cause trace that justified the fix.
  - **Fail signal:** Edits are described without linking to a criterion, or edits are made to items that did not fail any criterion ("improving" passing content).

### Correctness

- [ ] **EVAL-O06:** Root-cause traces identify SKILL.md instructions, not symptoms
  - **Test:** For each traced failure, the root cause names a specific instruction in SKILL.md (or its absence) — not a description of the wrong output.
  - **Fail signal:** Any trace entry describes what was wrong in the output without naming the SKILL.md instruction that should have prevented it.

- [ ] **EVAL-O07:** Fixes address the structural root cause, not the symptom
  - **Test:** For each SKILL.md edit, verify the edit changes an instruction (adds a missing check, clarifies ambiguous guidance, removes a wrong directive) rather than adding boilerplate that papers over the symptom.
  - **Fail signal:** An edit adds an instruction like "always produce complete output" without explaining how or when — a generic directive that addresses the symptom, not the cause.

- [ ] **EVAL-O08:** Pass rate trends upward or plateaus — never regresses across cycles
  - **Test:** If multiple cycles are run, the total pass rate (N/M criteria) in each cycle is ≥ the pass rate in the preceding cycle. A plateau (same score) triggers the ceiling check; a regression is a bug.
  - **Fail signal:** Any cycle shows a lower total pass rate than the preceding cycle without an explicit explanation that a fix introduced a new failure in a previously passing criterion.

- [ ] **EVAL-O09:** Execution-trace failures are attributed to SKILL.md dispatch instructions
  - **Test:** For each EVAL-E failure, the trace points to the specific dispatch instruction in SKILL.md that is wrong or absent, not to the subagent's behavior in isolation.
  - **Fail signal:** An EVAL-E failure is attributed to the subagent ("the subagent forgot to set model") without identifying which SKILL.md instruction should have required it.

- [ ] **EVAL-O10:** Structural score card distinguishes checklist tiers
  - **Test:** The structural cycle summary shows separate tallies for baseline, extended, EVAL-S, and flow-graph conformance — four distinct numbers.
  - **Fail signal:** The structural summary reports a single undifferentiated total without breaking out the four tier scores.

### Self-Consistency

- [ ] **EVAL-O11:** Score card totals match the sum of PASS cells in the grading table
  - **Test:** For the behavioral grading table, count the PASS cells per cycle row and verify the cycle total (N/M) matches the manual count. Repeat for the execution grading table if present.
  - **Fail signal:** The summary states "42/60 passed" but counting PASS cells in the table yields a different number.

- [ ] **EVAL-O12:** Every fix cycle described in prose is reflected in the score card
  - **Test:** Count the fix cycles mentioned in prose and verify the score card shows the same number of cycles with corresponding score progression entries.
  - **Fail signal:** Prose references a cycle the score card does not show, or the score card shows more cycles than the prose describes.

### Actionability

- [ ] **EVAL-O13:** Each surfaced blocker is specific and answerable
  - **Test:** When improve-skill surfaces a blocker (item still failing after two cycles), the blocker is phrased as a specific question or gap: "Line 42 says X but the criterion requires Y — this needs domain knowledge to resolve" rather than "criterion still failing."
  - **Fail signal:** Any blocker is described as "criterion N still failing" or equivalent with no statement of what information or decision is needed to resolve it.

- [ ] **EVAL-O14:** Proposed fixes are concrete edits, not vague suggestions
  - **Test:** For each fix proposed and applied, the edit is described as a specific change to specific content (line range, section, or instruction text) — not as a general recommendation.
  - **Fail signal:** Any fix is described as a general recommendation without a concrete edit shown or applied.

### Boundary Behavior

- [ ] **EVAL-O15:** `--structural-only` skips Pass 2 entirely
  - **Test:** When invoked with `--structural-only`, the output contains Pass 1 results and no behavioral grading table, no subagent dispatch for test prompts, and no mention of EVAL-O criteria results.
  - **Fail signal:** Pass 2 subagents are dispatched or grading table appears when `--structural-only` is set.

- [ ] **EVAL-O16:** `--behavioral-only` skips Pass 1 entirely
  - **Test:** When invoked with `--behavioral-only`, the output jumps directly to Pass 2. No structural cycle summary, no baseline checklist scoring, no SKILL.md structural edits appear.
  - **Fail signal:** Structural scoring or structural edits appear in the output when `--behavioral-only` is set.

- [ ] **EVAL-O17:** Missing EVAL.md triggers an offer, not a silent skip or error
  - **Test:** When no EVAL.md exists in the target skill directory, the output explicitly offers to generate one via `write-skill-eval` and states the consequence of declining (behavioral quality unverified).
  - **Fail signal:** Absence of EVAL.md causes Pass 2 to silently skip with no offer, or causes an error with no recovery path offered.

- [ ] **EVAL-O18:** Test prompts and EVAL.md criteria are never modified during behavioral pass
  - **Test:** After Pass 2 completes, the target skill's EVAL.md content is byte-for-byte identical to its pre-pass state. No criterion was softened, no test prompt was altered, no failing criterion was removed.
  - **Fail signal:** Any change to EVAL.md content is present after the behavioral pass.

- [ ] **EVAL-O19:** Ceiling condition is correctly identified and reported
  - **Test:** When the pass rate is stable across two consecutive behavioral cycles (same N/M), the output explicitly states that the skill has reached its ceiling for these prompts and lists remaining failures as blockers — rather than running a third cycle.
  - **Fail signal:** A third cycle runs after two consecutive cycles with identical pass rates, or the ceiling condition is reached but presented as a pass.

- [ ] **EVAL-O20:** Missing SKILL.md triggers loud failure and redirect
  - **Test:** If invoked for a skill that does not exist yet (no SKILL.md at the given path), the output fails loudly with the missing path named and redirects to `skill-creator` rather than attempting to score a missing file.
  - **Fail signal:** improve-skill attempts Pass 1 against a nonexistent SKILL.md, or errors without a clear redirect to the correct skill.

---

## Test Prompts

<!-- Diverse across personas: naive, experienced, adversarial, wrong-tool. -->
<!-- Prompts are written blind — without referencing the SKILL.md body. -->

### Naive User

#### Naive: No path given

**Prompt:** "improve this skill"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill halts to identify which skill file to act on, or proceeds blindly without the required artifact path.

#### Naive: Vague complaint

**Prompt:** "the skill isn't working well, can you fix it?"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill can extract actionable scope from an ambiguous complaint, or whether it silently picks a direction without clarifying what "not working well" means.

#### Naive: Quality pass before merge

**Prompt:** "review skill quality before I merge this PR"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill anchors on a concrete artifact path from context (staged files, working directory), or asks the user to specify one explicitly.

### Experienced User

#### Experienced: Targeted principle violation

**Prompt:** "improve `src/skills/docs/write-spec-docs/SKILL.md` — it's violating the policy-over-procedure principle: the body reads like a recipe with numbered steps instead of teaching judgment. Fix the structure without changing the intent."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill applies a specific design principle selectively rather than running a generic quality sweep that might introduce unwanted changes.

#### Experienced: Eval-anchored improvement loop

**Prompt:** "run an improvement loop on `src/skills/code/build-plan/SKILL.md` against its EVAL.md — stop only when all output criteria pass or you've exhausted 3 iterations. Show me the score delta per iteration."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill can execute a bounded, evidence-driven improvement cycle with explicit termination conditions and structured progress reporting.

#### Experienced: Companion-caused regression

**Prompt:** "I updated the `references/harness-mappings.yaml` companion for the `skill-router` skill. The skill itself wasn't touched, but outputs have regressed — the routing decisions are now worse. Improve the skill so it accounts for the new harness mappings without over-indexing on them."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill can detect quality regressions caused by companion file drift rather than direct SKILL.md edits, and scope improvement accordingly.

### Adversarial

#### Adversarial: Contradictory constraints

**Prompt:** "improve `synapse/skills/agent/agent-creator/SKILL.md` to score a 9/10 on every eval criterion, but don't change any of the instructions — only fix the formatting and frontmatter."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes that formatting-only changes cannot satisfy substantive eval criteria, and pushes back on the contradiction rather than producing a false passing score.

#### Adversarial: Compound scope overload

**Prompt:** "improve all the skills in `synapse/skills/protocol/` at once — each needs to pass its EVAL.md, follow the six design principles, and be ready for gatekeeper certification. Do it in one shot."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes that multi-artifact batch improvement in a single context is out of scope and redirects to a proper per-skill workflow.

### Wrong Tool

#### Wrong Tool: New skill authoring

**Prompt:** "I want to build a skill that helps with incident postmortems. Make it good quality from the start — solid design, eval criteria, the works."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes this is a creation task (belonging to `/skill-creator`) rather than an improvement task, and redirects rather than attempting to improve a skill that doesn't yet exist.

#### Wrong Tool: Gatekeeper certification

**Prompt:** "I've already improved `synapse/skills/meta/skill-router/SKILL.md` and it looks solid. Can you certify it and give me the APPROVE verdict so I can include it in my PR description?"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes that certification is owned by `/synapse-gatekeeper`, not itself, and redirects rather than issuing an unauthorized verdict.
